topic: General wave properties and wave motion

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Calculate the wavelength of a water wave given its speed and the number of waves passing a point over a specific time interval.
questions.reasoning_focus: Applying the wave equation v = fλ by first determining frequency from the given wave count and time.

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Identify the correct description of vibrations in a transverse wave and select a valid example of such a wave.
questions.reasoning_focus: Distinguishing between parallel and perpendicular vibrations relative to the direction of energy travel.

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Label a diagram showing circular waves on the surface of water with the correct terms for wavefront and wavelength.
questions.reasoning_focus: Identifying physical features of a wave on a visual representation of a disturbed surface.

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Determine the direction of movement of a floating object (cork) when transverse water waves pass through its position.
questions.reasoning_focus: Understanding that in transverse waves, the medium (or floating objects) moves perpendicular to the direction of wave travel.

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Determine the amplitude and frequency of a transverse wave using a displacement-time graph.
questions.reasoning_focus: Reading maximum displacement from a graph and calculating frequency as the reciprocal of the period.

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Calculate the frequency of light given its speed and its wavelength.
questions.reasoning_focus: Applying the mathematical relationship v = fλ to electromagnetic waves.

questions.topic: General wave properties and wave motion
questions.question_summary: Calculate the frequency of monochromatic red light given its wavelength in air and the speed of light.
questions.reasoning_focus: Applying the wave equation to find frequency for a specific color of light.
